title: $hits
text: $hits Return the number of occurrences in the hitlist. $hits { ( Entity ) } Parameters Table 1. Parameters Parameter Data Type Description Entity String Entity name; optional. If omitted, the current entity is used. Return Values Total number of occurrences in the hitlist. An empty string ("") if an error occurred. $procerror contains a negative value that identifies the exact error. Table 2. Values of $procerror Commonly Returned Following $hits Value Error constant Meaning <UIOSERR_*> (-2 through -12) Errors during database I/O. <UNETERR_*> (-16 through -30) Errors during network I/O. -1102 <UPROCERR_ENTITY> The entity name provided is not a valid name or the entity is not painted on the component. Use Allowed in all component types. Description If the DBMS you are accessing supports a stepped hitlist, any performance benefits derived from use of the stepped hitlist are lost when you use $hits , since building the complete hitlist can be quite time-consuming. Table 3. Statements that Change $hits Event Action clear Sets $hits to 0 . release Sets $hits to 0 . discard Reduces $hits by the number of discarded occurrences. Note: The Debugger does not build the complete hitlist if you examine $hits . Instead, it returns the value in $curhits . Example: Using $hits The following example shows the exec operation of a form. This ProcScript copies a value into the form which defines a profile, then retrieves all the companies which match that profile. The assignment statement copies the total number of hits which match the profile to a dummy field in the footer frame on the form: operation exec COMPNAME = $1 retrieve if ($status < 0) message "Retrieve error" exit (0) endif TOTSEL.TRAILER = $hits(COMPANY) display COMPNAME end; exec Copy Related Links lookup Count the number of occurrences that match the current profile. retrieve Activate the read trigger for the first outermost entity and all related entities, or for a specific entity. setocc Make a specific occurrence the current occurrence. $curhits Return the number of occurrences in the hitlist. $dbocc Return the sequence number of the current occurrence within the set of occurrences retrieved from the database.
